zh.Montgomery, Wales: 英國威爾斯蒙格莫利夏爾蒙哥馬利

zh-hans.Montgomery, Wales: 英国威尔斯蒙格莫利夏爾蒙哥馬利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nicolas ap Philip于1330年出生时，他的父亲Philip ap Eldir31岁，母亲Gwladus ferch Dafydd31岁。他于大约1375年与Jonet ferch Gruffudd在英国威尔斯蒙格莫利夏爾蒙哥馬利结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: occupational name for a cobbler.
Slovenian: nickname for a talkative person, from lapati ‘to babble’, or a topographic name from dialect lap ‘abyss’. Compare Lapp 4.

variants.name: Lapp
meanings: German, English, and Dutch: from Middle High German and Middle English lappe, Middle Dutch lap ‘cloth, patch, rag’, a metonymic occupational name for a mender of clothes or shoes, or a nickname given to someone who wore ragged clothes or garments with loose folds, flaps, or hanging sleeves, or who had fleshy folds of skin on his face, neck, or body.

German: nickname for a simple-minded person, from Middle Low German lappe ‘fool’. This surname (in any of the two possible senses; see also 1 above) is also found in France (mainly Alsace, also Lorraine).
English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French lappe ‘burdock’, perhaps for someone with a prickly character.

nameMeanings.name: Philip
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Varughese, Mathai, Babu, Reji, Anil, Binu, Neena, Prema, Raju, Reena, Susheela, Aleykutty.

Scottish, English, German, Dutch, and Jewish: from the Greek personal name Philippos (from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’). In the New Testament this name is borne by one of the apostles; it was also borne by various other early Christian saints. It owes part of its popularity to the medieval romances about Alexander the Great, whose father was Philip of Macedon. As a Jewish name, it represents a borrowing of the personal name from Christians. As a Highland Scottish surname, it represents an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhilib ‘son of Philip’. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Spanish Felipe , Catalan Felip, Polish, Czech, Slovak, Slovenian or Croatian Filip , Albanian Filipi , and their derivatives (see examples at Philips ). The name Philip is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Philipose and Pothen ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Phillip .
French (southern) and Breton (Finistère): variant of   Philippe  .

variants.name: McKillip
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhilib ‘son of Philip’ (see Philip ). See also McPhillips .

variants.name: Felipe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Juana, Miguel, Orlando, Pedro, Angel, Carlos, Emilio, Jesus.
Spanish: from the personal name Felipe, an equivalent of Philippe .

variants.name: Lips
meanings: North German, Flemish, and Dutch: from a shortened form of the personal name Philips, from Latin Philippus (see Philip ).

variants.name: Lipps
meanings: North German: from a shortened form of the personal name Philipps, from Latin Philippus (see Philip ).

variants.name: Pepin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Camille, Emile, Pierre, Gilles, Lucien, Normand, Alcide, Fernand, Jacques, Marcel.

French (Pépin) and English: from the ancient Germanic personal name Pipin, of uncertain, probably onomatopoeic origin. The name was borne by several Frankish kings, most notably Pepin le Bref, father of Charlemagne, and remained popular throughout the early Middle Ages.
English: from the unrecorded Anglo-Norman French and Middle English personal names Pipin, Pepin, Pipun, pet forms of Philip . Compare Pipe .

variants.name: Lippens
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alfons, Alphons.  French Andre.
Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from a shortened form of the personal name   Philip  .

variants.name: Philbin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Philbín, ‘son of Philbin (Irish Philbín or Filibín)’, a pet form of Philip .

variants.name: Lipman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Mayer, Chaim, Dov, Isadore, Jakob, Meyer, Pinchas.

Dutch: habitational name from a farm named Lipman or perhaps Lippink, referring to a certain Lippo and his kin. Lippo is a pet form of an ancient Germanic personal name composed with the element leiba ‘offspring, son’, or of the personal name Philip .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish personal name Lipman, derived from Middle High German words liep ‘dear, beloved’ + man ‘man’.

variants.name: Philipose
meanings: Indian (Kerala): from the personal name Philipose, derived from Greek Philippos (see Philip ), found among Christians in southern India. — Note: Since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, this name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Philibert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Fernand, Mirielle, Pascale, Pierrot.

French: from the personal name Philibert, from ancient Germanic Filuberht, composed of the elements fila ‘much, very’ + berht ‘bright’, which was borne by a 7th-century Christian saint, abbot of Jumièges. The spelling has been influenced by personal names of Greek origin based on philein ‘to love’, e.g. Philip .

nameMeanings.name: Nicolas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Late Greek. See Nicholas .

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nicolao
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

en.lifeSummary: When Nicolas ap Philip was born in 1330, his father, Philip ap Eldir, was 31 and his mother, Gwladus ferch Dafydd, was 31. He married Jonet ferch Gruffudd about 1375, in Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter.

fr.Montgomery, Wales: Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicolas ap Philip est né en 1330, son père, Philip ap Eldir, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Gwladus ferch Dafydd, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Jonet ferch Gruffudd environ 1375, à Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nicolas ap Philip 1330 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Philip ap Eldir 31 und seine Mutter Gwladus ferch Dafydd 31. Er hat um 1375 in Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom Jonet ferch Gruffudd geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter.

ht.Montgomery, Wales: Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Pays de Galles, United Kingdom

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nicolas ap Philip è nato il nel 1330, il suo padre, Philip ap Eldir, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Gwladus ferch Dafydd, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Jonet ferch Gruffudd circa 1375, nel Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia.

ja.Montgomery, Wales: イギリスウェールズモントゴメリシュアイアモントゴメリー
ja.lifeSummary: Nicolas ap Philipが1330年に生まれた時，父Philip ap Eldirは31歳，母Gwladus ferch Dafyddは31歳であった。約1375年にイギリスウェールズモントゴメリシュアイアモントゴメリーでJonet ferch Gruffuddと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Nicolas ap Philip(이)가 1330년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Philip ap Eldir(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Gwladus ferch Dafydd(은)는 31세였다. 그는 대략 1375년 영국 웨일스 몽고머이실르 몽고메리에서 Jonet ferch Gruffudd(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

Ele casou-se com Jonet ferch Gruffudd aproximadamente 1375, em Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Gales, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicolas ap Philip родился в 1330 г., его отцу по имени Philip ap Eldir было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Gwladus ferch Dafydd, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Jonet ferch Gruffudd около 1375 г. в местности монтгомири, монтгомиришир, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nicolas ap Philip nació en 1330, su papá, Philip ap Eldir, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Gwladus ferch Dafydd, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Jonet ferch Gruffudd aproximadamente 1375, en Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija.
